items.verse: ٱصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَٱذْكُرْ عَبْدَنَا دَاوُۥدَ ذَا ٱلْأَيْدِ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌ
items.verseEn: Be patient over what they say and remember Our servant, David, the possessor of strength; indeed, he was one who repeatedly turned back [to Allāh].

items.verse: وَوَرِثَ سُلَيْمَـٰنُ دَاوُۥدَ ۖ وَقَالَ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ عُلِّمْنَا مَنطِقَ ٱلطَّيْرِ وَأُوتِينَا مِن كُلِّ شَىْءٍ ۖ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْمُبِينُ
items.verseEn: And Solomon inherited David. He said, "O people, we have been taught the language of birds, and we have been given from all things. Indeed, this is evident bounty."

items.verse: وَٱذْكُرْ فِى ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صَادِقَ ٱلْوَعْدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولًۭا نَّبِيًّۭا
items.verseEn: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet.
And indeed, Jonah was among the messengers.

categories.name: صِفَاتُ الرِّيَاحِ فِي الْقُرْآنِ
categories.nameEn: Adjectival descriptions of wind in the Quran

items.verse: وَلِسُلَيْمَـٰنَ ٱلرِّيحَ عَاصِفَةًۭ تَجْرِى بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ إِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلَّتِى بَـٰرَكْنَا فِيهَا ۚ وَكُنَّا بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.

items.verse: وَفِى عَادٍ إِذْ أَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلرِّيحَ ٱلْعَقِيمَ
items.verseEn: And in ʿAad [was a sign], when We sent against them the barren wind.

items.verse: أَمْ أَمِنتُمْ أَن يُعِيدَكُمْ فِيهِ تَارَةً أُخْرَىٰ فَيُرْسِلَ عَلَيْكُمْ قَاصِفًۭا مِّنَ ٱلرِّيحِ فَيُغْرِقَكُم بِمَا كَفَرْتُمْ ۙ ثُمَّ لَا تَجِدُوا۟ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا بِهِۦ تَبِيعًۭا
items.verseEn: Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back into it [i.e., the sea] another time and send upon you a hurricane of wind and drown you for what you denied? Then you would not find for yourselves against Us an avenger.

items.verse: فَسَخَّرْنَا لَهُ ٱلرِّيحَ تَجْرِى بِأَمْرِهِۦ رُخَآءً حَيْثُ أَصَابَ
items.verseEn: So We subjected to him the wind blowing by his command, gently, wherever he directed,

categories.name: أَوْصَافٌ لِلْعِقَابِ أَوِ الطَّرْدِ
categories.nameEn: Words describing states of punishment or expulsion

items.verse: قَالَ ٱخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مَذْءُومًۭا مَّدْحُورًۭا ۖ لَّمَن تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
items.verseEn: [Allāh] said, "Depart from it [i.e., Paradise], reproached and expelled. Whoever follows you among them - I will surely fill Hell with you, all together."

items.verse: مَّن كَانَ يُرِيدُ ٱلْعَاجِلَةَ عَجَّلْنَا لَهُۥ فِيهَا مَا نَشَآءُ لِمَن نُّرِيدُ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا لَهُۥ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَىٰهَا مَذْمُومًۭا مَّدْحُورًۭا
items.verseEn: Whoever should desire the immediate - We hasten for him from it what We will to whom We intend. Then We have made for him Hell, which he will [enter to] burn, censured and banished.

items.verse: قَالُوا۟ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ يَـٰنُوحُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَرْجُومِينَ
items.verseEn: They said, "If you do not desist, O Noah, you will surely be of those who are stoned."
And [by] the sea set on fire,

categories.name: أَصْوَاتٌ غَيْرُ بَشَرِيَّةٍ
categories.nameEn: Sounds from non-human or inanimate sources

items.verse: فَأَخْرَجَ لَهُمْ عِجْلًۭا جَسَدًۭا لَّهُۥ خُوَارٌۭ فَقَالُوا۟ هَـٰذَآ إِلَـٰهُكُمْ وَإِلَـٰهُ مُوسَىٰ فَنَسِىَ
items.verseEn: And he extracted for them [the statue of] a calf which had a lowing sound, and they said, "This is your god and the god of Moses, but he forgot."

items.verse: إِذَا رَأَتْهُم مِّن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍۢ سَمِعُوا۟ لَهَا تَغَيُّظًۭا وَزَفِيرًۭا
items.verseEn: When it [i.e., the Hellfire] sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.

items.verse: فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ شَقُوا۟ فَفِى ٱلنَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيهَا زَفِيرٌۭ وَشَهِيقٌ
items.verseEn: As for those who were [destined to be] wretched, they will be in the Fire. For them therein is [violent] exhaling and inhaling.

items.verse: وَأَمَّا عَادٌۭ فَأُهْلِكُوا۟ بِرِيحٍۢ صَرْصَرٍ عَاتِيَةٍۢ
items.verseEn: And as for ʿAad, they were destroyed by a screaming, violent wind

harf.hint: The struggle and toil inherent in human existence.
harf.verse: 90:4 — We have certainly created man into hardship.

deduction.title: The Prayer in the Sanctuary
deduction.intro: In a sacred sanctuary, a devoted servant of Allah, advanced in age, offered a heartfelt plea. This individual yearned for an heir to carry on a legacy of faith, despite circumstances that made it seem impossible. A miraculous answer came, but it was accompanied by a unique sign to affirm the divine promise. Who is this patient soul whose quiet prayer was answered in a wondrous way?
clues: I was a guardian and a caretaker, entrusted with a sacred responsibility within my community.
I watched over a righteous child who received provisions directly from Allah, which strengthened my own faith in His power.
In my old age, and with my wife being barren, I secretly called upon my Lord for a righteous heir.
While I was standing in prayer in the sanctuary, angels brought me the glad tidings of a son, whose name was chosen by Allah Himself.
I asked for a sign to reassure my heart, and it was granted to me in a peculiar way.
My sign was that I was unable to speak to people for three nights, though I was perfectly healthy, communicating only through gestures.

trial.label: What did I pray for?
options: To see Allah on the mountain
For my son to be saved from sacrifice
A righteous heir in old age
For my people to be saved from a flood
trial.answer: A righteous heir in old age

location.label: Key Element
options: A hand turning white without disease
A she-camel emerging from a rock
The inability to speak for three nights
A fire made cool and peaceful
A staff that split the sea
location.answer: The inability to speak for three nights

deduction.arabic: قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةًۭ ۚ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَـٰثَ لَيَالٍۢ سَوِيًّۭا
deduction.verse: [Zechariah] said, "My Lord, make for me a sign." He said, "Your sign is that you will not speak to the people for three nights, [being] sound." (19:10)

translations: And with Him (are the) keys (of) the unseen no (one) knows them except Him And He knows
what (is) in the land and in the sea And not falls of any leaf
but He knows it And not a grain in the darkness[es] (of) the earth and not
moist and not dry but (is) in a Record Clear
scramble.hint: This verse speaks about Allah's exclusive and absolute knowledge of all that is hidden.
scramble.arabic: ۞ وَعِندَهُۥ مَفَاتِحُ ٱلْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍۢ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا رَطْبٍۢ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
words: ۞ وَعِندَهُۥ مَفَاتِحُ ٱلْغَيْبِ لَا يَعْلَمُهَآ إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ وَيَعْلَمُ
مَا فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۚ وَمَا تَسْقُطُ مِن وَرَقَةٍ
إِلَّا يَعْلَمُهَا وَلَا حَبَّةٍۢ فِى ظُلُمَـٰتِ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا
رَطْبٍۢ وَلَا يَابِسٍ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍۢ مُّبِينٍۢ
scramble.verseEn: And with Him are the keys of the unseen; none knows them except Him. And He knows what is on the land and in the sea. Not a leaf falls but that He knows it. And no grain is there within the darknesses of the earth and no moist or dry [thing] but that it is [written] in a clear record.
